projects.title: Artem Health Care
projects.summary: A patient management platform where speed and clarity are patient-safety features, not nice-to-haves.
projects.problem: Care teams were losing time navigating a legacy interface with inconsistent patterns, slow record lookups, and forms that weren't accessible to keyboard or screen-reader users. Every extra second spent hunting for a patient record was a second not spent on care — and the inconsistent form patterns meant new staff faced a real learning curve just to use the software.
approach: Audited every core flow — record lookup, intake forms, scheduling — against WCAG 2.1 AA, cataloguing every keyboard trap and unlabeled control.
Designed a token-based Angular + PrimeNG component library so buttons, form fields, and data tables shared one visual and behavioral contract.
Rebuilt record search with debounced queries and skeleton states so the interface felt responsive even while data loaded.
Ran the new patterns past clinical staff in short usability passes before rolling them out platform-wide.
projects.role_detail: Led UI architecture and design system implementation, translating Figma designs into a reusable Angular + PrimeNG component library and auditing every core flow for WCAG compliance.
projects.impact: Reduced average time-to-record lookup and standardized over a dozen previously inconsistent form patterns into a single accessible pattern library used across the platform.

results.label: Form patterns unified into one library
Core flows audited for WCAG 2.1 AA

projects.title: Compito
projects.summary: A task and project management workspace rebuilt around focus — less chrome, more signal.
projects.problem: The existing board-based workflow buried important tasks under visual noise, and the component library had drifted out of sync with the Figma source of truth — every new feature meant re-deciding basic interaction patterns because there was no longer a single source of truth to build from.
approach: Re-established a single shared component library synced with Figma via Zeplin handoff, so design and code stopped drifting apart.
Rebuilt the core board, card, and modal components in React with Tailwind CSS, stripping decorative chrome in favor of clear visual hierarchy.
Layered optimistic UI updates onto drag-and-drop actions so the board felt instant even before the network request resolved.
Introduced a lightweight design-token layer in Tailwind config so spacing and color decisions stayed consistent as new views were added.
projects.role_detail: Rebuilt the core board, card, and modal components in React with Tailwind CSS, and re-established a single shared component library synced with Figma via Zeplin handoff.
projects.impact: Cut UI implementation time for new features by reusing a consistent component set, and improved perceived performance through optimistic UI updates on drag-and-drop actions.

results.label: Core components rebuilt as the shared foundation
Single source of truth restored between Figma and code
Perceived delay on drag-and-drop, via optimistic UI

projects.title: Valyxto
projects.summary: A fintech dashboard where dense data had to become scannable without losing precision.
projects.problem: Users needed to parse large tables of financial data quickly, but the existing layout treated every data point with equal visual weight, making priority information hard to find — a dashboard that shows everything at once shows nothing clearly.
approach: Reworked the information hierarchy using typography scale, color, and progressive disclosure so priority figures stood out without hiding supporting detail.
Implemented the resulting components in Angular with PrimeNG and Bootstrap utilities, keeping the table and chart primitives consistent across every report view.
Established a single data-table pattern — sorting, pagination, and density controls — reused across every report instead of rebuilt per screen.
Validated the new hierarchy against real report data, not lorem-ipsum placeholders, to make sure the design held up at production density.
projects.role_detail: Redesigned the information hierarchy using typography scale, color, and progressive disclosure, and implemented the resulting components in Angular with PrimeNG and Bootstrap utilities.
projects.impact: Improved scan-ability of core dashboards through a clearer visual hierarchy and consistent data-table patterns reused across every report view.

results.label: Unified data-table pattern reused across all reports
Visual hierarchy tiers — priority, supporting, reference
Report views migrated to the new pattern
